svg.text: Conv_Simple / CV-101 (garage conveyor) — Generated from open_items.yaml — the formal field-verification docket

Trace conductors on the bench; verify Q1 poles 1-6 actual locations.

Determine bench PE bonding strategy; verify RFI jumpers per

Trace the PLC output block; confirm zero hardwired leads to any GS10

wires.yaml convention + the E-001 key). E-005 renumbered W200..W205 →

RESOLVED (2026-07-11): no separate motor contactor — the MLC control
relay IS the single-phase drive-supply switch (2 NO contacts). See
Confirm whether a separate motor contactor exists between CB1 and
VFD1; the device labeled 'MLC' (Q1) is a Schneider TeSys CA3KN22BD
CONTROL RELAY — it has no main power poles.

terminals are used (speed reference +10V/AI/ACM vs feedback
AO1/DO1/DOC) and to what. Confirm nothing on the top run/DI row.

Siemens CPU 1212C AC/DC/RLY (S7-1200) observed on the panel (photo
Confirm whether the Siemens CPU 1212C controls any CV-101 function
before referencing it on any CV-101 sheet; not drawn until evidenced.
Device labeled "PMC 192.x" observed on the panel (photo wire_2).
Identify the device (network device / meter?) and its relationship, if
DC +/- distribution block: which color (blue vs white) is +24V vs 0V,
circuit count, PS1 branch fusing; PLUS PS1's AC input source — which
leg/circuit feeds L and N (120 V leg vs the 230 V 1φ supply) is not
Trace/meter the distribution block conductors against PS1 +V/-V
output; confirm color-to-polarity mapping, circuit count, and any
branch fusing (photo wire_1/wire_2). Separately, identify the branch
circuit feeding PS1.L/PS1.N and its voltage (single leg of the 230 V
1φ supply, or a dedicated 120 V circuit).
MLC (Q1) aux contacts (full-res photo): coil A1/A2 wired + NO contacts
13-14 & 43-44 in use confirmed; NC 21-22/31-32 appear unused. Trace
where 13-14 and 43-44 land in the control circuit.
Trace each aux contact to its destination in the control circuit;
confirm function (e.g., interlock feedback, seal-in, indicator).
GS10 is a standard 230 V 1φ-in / 230 V 3φ-out drive; the earlier
reported 480 V was a misstatement. E-003 letters 230 V 3φ output.
Closed by technician confirmation. Exact GS10 model/frame still
• RESOLVED items stay listed (grayed, not deleted) for traceability — see the item text for the resolving evidence and date.
• Closing an open item requires evidence — a repo artifact citation or a bench measurement — not a re-assertion (see the model discipline note on E-001).
